problem_statement:
Separate information panel from editable lyrics and bookmarks
Original [issue 1068](https://code.google.com/p/quodlibet/issues/detail?id=1068) created by nick.boultbee on 2012-11-24T21:45:42.000Z:

Currently viewing/editing lyrics and viewing/editing bookmarks both are on tabs under the context menu item entitled "Information".

It's misleading that an informational panel is the only place to edit these two pieces of song metadata.

See also Issue #940, as these could be solved together.

patch:
diff --git a/.ci/macos-install.sh b/.ci/macos-install.sh
index 60b61400cd..fe694a3ac9 100755
--- a/.ci/macos-install.sh
+++ b/.ci/macos-install.sh
@@ -7,4 +7,4 @@ hdiutil attach -noverify -readonly -mountpoint _mount ql.dmg
 cp -R _mount/QuodLibet.app "$TMPDIR/_app"
 hdiutil detach -force _mount
 RUN="$TMPDIR/_app/Contents/MacOS/run"
-$RUN -m pip install "ruff==0.7.3" "pytest==7.4.4" pytest-faulthandler flaky "coverage[toml]"
+$RUN -m pip install "ruff==0.7.3" "pytest==7.4.4" pytest-faulthandler flaky "coverage[toml]" "pypresence>=4.6.1" "regex>=2025.9.18"
diff --git a/.github/workflows/main.yml b/.github/workflows/main.yml
index 93fe3d0dde..71e2401fb3 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/main.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/main.yml
@@ -140,13 +140,15 @@ jobs:
             python3-polib \
             python3-pytest \
             python3-setuptools \
+            python3-regex \
             xvfb
 
           python3 -m pip install --user --upgrade \
             PyVirtualDisplay \
             pytest-faulthandler \
             "ruff==0.7.3" \
-            flaky
+            flaky \
+            "pypresence>=4.6.1"
 
       - name: Run tests
         run: |
diff --git a/dev-utils/flatpak/Makefile b/dev-utils/flatpak/Makefile
index afbe2cd67a..af28e7e739 100644
--- a/dev-utils/flatpak/Makefile
+++ b/dev-utils/flatpak/Makefile
@@ -6,6 +6,7 @@ PLUGINS_DEPENDS := \
 	musicbrainzngs \

+    def truncate_unicode_text(text: str, num: int) -> str:
+        """
+        Truncate a given unicode string to a string that is less than the given
+        number of unicode code points that has an affixed truncation indicator

+        fit to publish to rich presence without error.

+        :param text: A unicode string to truncate.
+        :type text: str
+        :param num: The number of unicode code points to truncate to.
+        :type num: int
+        :return: The truncated string in UTF-16.
+        :rtype: str

+        # Return the same text [in UTF-16] if it doesn't need to be truncated
+        if _utf16_cu_len(text) <= num:
+            return str(text.encode("utf-16"), encoding="utf-16")
+
+        # Cache the byte lengths of the truncation indicator/suffix
+        trunc_char_len: int = _utf16_cu_len(DISCORD_RP_DETAILS_TRUNC_SUFFIX)
+
+        # Factor in the code point length of the truncation character
+        clen: int = trunc_char_len
+        # Iterate through unicode graphemes and build the string to return
+        x: str = ""
+        for grapheme_match in GRAPHEME_PATTERN.finditer(text):

+            # Append the number of code points for the grapheme

+            clen += _utf16_cu_len(grapheme)
+            # Break when the total found code point length exceeds the limit
+            if clen > num:
+                break

+

+    _utf16_cu_len,

+
+
+class TDiscordStatusMessage(PluginTestCase):
+    def setUp(self):
+        pass
+

+        pass

+    def test_unicode_truncation(self):
+        truncate_func = DiscordStatusMessage.truncate_unicode_text
+        D_MAX_CU = DISCORD_RP_DETAILS_MAX_CODEUNITS
+
+        # Truncation of simple strings to limit
+        x_str = "x" * 145
+        # Truncation of heterogeneous strings to limit
+        het_str = ("f" * 30) + ("🇦🇶" * 30) + ("🏴‍☠️" * 30)
+
+        assert _utf16_cu_len(truncate_func(x_str, D_MAX_CU)) <= D_MAX_CU
+        assert _utf16_cu_len(truncate_func(het_str, D_MAX_CU)) <= D_MAX_CU
+
+        # Truncation of strings less than the length of the given limit returns
+        # the original string content
+        sub_max_len = 56
+        sub_max_str = "Q" * sub_max_len
+
+        sub_max_res = truncate_func(sub_max_str, D_MAX_CU)
+        assert _utf16_cu_len(sub_max_str) == _utf16_cu_len(sub_max_res)
+        assert str(sub_max_str.encode("utf-16"), encoding="utf-16") == sub_max_res
problem_statement:
Add plugin for for rich presence in Discord
Hi there, I was wondering if there would be any interest in adding Discord integration into the player? I noticed there's a couple different IM clients that quodlibet can pass information to and it would be great if Discord could be added for that purpose as well, as it's very popular and various other players support it either natively or through plug-ins.

I have very little coding knowledge but as far as I can tell there is a fully-featured SDK for this sort of thing here: https://discord.com/developers/docs/game-sdk/activities

Love the player in any case!

I think you should handle the dependency following this pattern: https://github.com/quodlibet/quodlibet/blob/225d29a40c2af7023d9162a1395719d72bd4ceb1/quodlibet/ext/events/mqtt.py#L22-L26

Start Quodlibet and play some of my fave stations (those are present)

---------------

The "Would you like to load...." prompt shows every time I start QL.

### Which version of Quod Libet?
4.6.0

Debian Trixie (testing)

Not audio related.

----------------------
I think this behavior started with 4.6.0, but I am not 100% sure; backdating to an older version is troublesome for dependency reasons.

problem_statement:
Quodlibet uses images that don't match the configured filename
Steps to reproduce
------------------
Configure quodlibet like:
```
[albumart]
filename = folder.png
force_filename = true
prefer_embedded = false
```


Expected Output
---------------
Quodlibet should only use files named `folder.png` as the cover image.


Actual Output
-------------
It looks like it picked an image with a different filename:
```
D: [0001.595] util.cover.manager.run: Found local cover from FilesystemCover: <_io.BufferedReader name='/home/dseomn/Music/master/Lizzo/Special/front.jpg'>
```

There's no folder.png in that directory, so it should have shown no image.


Test System
-----------

### Which version of Quod Libet?
4.6.0-1

### Which operating system 
Debian testing


### If it's audio-related, what back-end?
N/A


Additional Information
----------------------
This behavior made it harder to figure out what was going on in https://github.com/quodlibet/quodlibet/issues/3287#issuecomment-1944864059.

problem_statement:
Delay watching for file changes to after start of GUI
The proposed change
-------------------
When the "Watch directories for changes" option in the "Library" tab of the settings is activated the file watches are being created before the GUI runs. This happens irrespective of the "Scan library on start" option. On a system with slow IO (e.g. music library is stored on a slow NAS) walking the file tree takes a considerable amount of time before the user can start using the music player.

Delaying the traversal of the file tree for file watches to after the initialization of `QuodLibetWindow` drastically improves the startup time on such systems. A very crude diff that works quite well on my machine looks like this:

````diff
diff --git a/quodlibet/library/__init__.py b/quodlibet/library/__init__.py
index 86059f6b9..41f1e1bb1 100644
--- a/quodlibet/library/__init__.py
+++ b/quodlibet/library/__init__.py
@@ -31,8 +31,7 @@ def init(cache_fn=None):
     """
 
     SongFileLibrary.librarian = SongLibrary.librarian = SongLibrarian()
-    watch = config.getboolean("library", "watch")
-    library = SongFileLibrary("main", watch_dirs=get_scan_dirs() if watch else [])
+    library = SongFileLibrary(name="main", watch_dirs=[])
     if cache_fn:
         library.load(cache_fn)
     return library
diff --git a/quodlibet/main.py b/quodlibet/main.py
index d7e622a9f..6b9dc6fbf 100644
--- a/quodlibet/main.py
+++ b/quodlibet/main.py
@@ -147,6 +147,12 @@ def main(argv=None):
         restore_cb=lambda: GLib.idle_add(exec_commands, priority=GLib.PRIORITY_HIGH),
     )
 
+    from quodlibet.util.library import get_scan_dirs
+
+    watch = config.getboolean("library", "watch")
+    if watch:
+        app.library.start_watching(get_scan_dirs())
+
     app.player_options = PlayerOptions(window)
 
     from quodlibet.qltk.window import Window
````

Benefits of this change
-----------------------
The startup of Quod Libet becomes much faster for users that want to watch for file changes in libraries that are slow to traverse (e.g. large libraries stored on a slow NAS). On my machine it reduced the startup time by **84.5%**.
